mnemonics.title: Inner Product Properties
mnemonics.content: BSP: Bilinear - works with both inputs; Symmetric - swap doesn't change; Positive definite - zero only at zero. Think 'BSP - Best Spatial Properties'.

mnemonics.title: Cauchy-Schwarz记忆
mnemonics.content: |⟨u,v⟩| ≤ ||u|| × ||v||. The absolute value of inner product is bounded by product of norms. 'CS: Can't Surpass Final Value'.

mnemonics.title: Gram-Schmidt Process
mnemonics.content: Orthogonalize sequentially: For each new vector, subtract all its projections onto previously computed orthogonal vectors. 'GS: Get Stuff Quantized, Subtract Projections'.

mnemonics.title: Orthonormal Definition
mnemonics.content: ⟨vᵢ,vⱼ⟩ = δᵢⱼ where δᵢⱼ = 1 if i=j, 0 otherwise. 'OrthoNormal: One (1) and Zero (0) - ON'O'.

acronyms.usage: The three defining properties of any inner product on a real vector space
Set of vectors that are both orthogonal (O) and normalized (N) - unit length and mutually perpendicular
Method to convert any basis into an orthonormal basis through sequential orthogonalization

visualTips.visualization: Think of ⟨u,v⟩ as the length of the projection of u onto v, multiplied by ||v||. Geometrically, it's like shining light from u onto v and measuring the 'shadow' length.

visualTips.visualize: Perpendicular vectors in 2D/3D. Imagine right angles at 90°. Two vectors pointing in completely different directions have zero overlap (zero inner product).
The inner product cannot exceed the product of lengths - think of the worst case as both vectors pointing in exactly the same direction (maximum alignment).
The 'shortest path' principle: the direct route (||u+v||) is never longer than going one after the other (||u||+||v||). Like taking a direct flight vs. two connecting flights.
Building a staircase: Each new step (vector) is made perpendicular to all previous steps by removing the 'shadow' each one casts on it.

analogies.analogy: Like 'compatibility score' between two vectors - high score means they point in similar directions, zero means they're completely different/opposite.
Two people with completely different skills - their collaboration produces zero output in overlapping areas.
The 'size' or 'strength' of a vector, like the length of an arrow. You measure it by how much the vector contributes to itself.
The correlation between two variables can never exceed the product of their standard deviations - you can't be more correlated than perfectly correlated.
Converting a messy team into a well-coordinated team where everyone has a unique, non-overlapping role - making everyone orthogonal to each other.
